zh.lifeSummary: 當Ecuyer Pierre I de Quincy et de Montauban於1255年出生在法國豪-德-福朗斯諾爾德坎布萊時，他的父親Raoul de Falvy35歲，母親Marguerite36歲。他和Marie de Bazentin至少有1個女兒。他去世在法國豪-德-福朗斯索姆法爾維。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey

English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Quincy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in France deriving their names from the Gallo-Roman personal name Quintus, meaning ‘fifth(-born)’ + the locative suffix -acum. The earliest bearer of the name in England, that of Saer de Quincy, ancestor of the earls of Winchester, was from Cuinchy in Pas-de-Calais, but the name may have been reinforced by immigrants from Quincy-sous-Sénard in Seine-et-Oise or Quincy-Voisins in Seine-et-Marne. A family of the name de Quincy held the title Earl of Winchester 1207–65. Descendants of this family later bore the name Quincey up to the time of the English essayist Thomas De Quincey (1785–1859), who changed his name from Quincey to De Quincey in 1796.

History: The American Quincy family were established in MA by Edmund Quincy in 1633. Fifth in descent was Josiah Quincy (1744–75), a leading patriot, who was sent to England to argue the colonists’ case in 1774. His son Josiah (1772–1864) was a powerful opponent of slavery, president of Harvard, and mayor of Boston, a post also held by several of his descendants. The traditional pronunciation is “Quinzy”.

nameMeanings.name: et
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: West Frisian and Dutch: from the Frisian personal name Ente, possibly from an ancient Germanic name composed with Old High German ando ‘breath, wrath’.
Dutch (Van der Ent): topographic name for someone living at the end of the village.

nameMeanings.name: Montauban
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Spanish (Montalbán): habitational name from Montalbán de Córdoba, from Latin montem albanum ‘white mountain’.

nameMeanings.name: Pierre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Ecuyer Pierre I de Quincy et de Montauban was born in 1255, in Cambrai, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France, his father, Raoul de Falvy, was 35 and his mother, Marguerite, was 36. He had at least 1 daughter with Marie de Bazentin. He died in Falvy, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ecuyer Pierre I de Quincy et de Montauban est né en 1255, à Cambrai, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France, son père, Raoul de Falvy, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Marguerite, avait 36 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Marie de Bazentin. Il est décédé à Falvy, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ecuyer Pierre I de Quincy et de Montauban 1255 in Cambrai, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Raoul de Falvy 35 und seine Mutter Marguerite 36. Er hatte mit Marie de Bazentin mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb in Falvy, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ecuyer Pierre I de Quincy et de Montauban è nato il nel 1255 nel Cambrai, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Francia, il suo padre, Raoul de Falvy, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Marguerite, aveva 36 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Marie de Bazentin. Morì nel Falvy, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia.

ja.Falvy, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France: フランスハウトス-デ-フランスソンメファルビー
ja.lifeSummary: Ecuyer Pierre I de Quincy et de Montaubanが1255年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスノードカンブレイで生まれた時，父Raoul de Falvyは35歳，母Margueriteは36歳であった。彼はMarie de Bazentinとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼はフランスハウトス-デ-フランスソンメファルビーで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Ecuyer Pierre I de Quincy et de Montauban(이)가 1255년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 노르드 캄브라이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Raoul de Falvy(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Marguerite(은)는 36세였다. 그는 Marie de Bazentin(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 솜 팔비에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ecuyer Pierre I de Quincy et de Montauban nasceu em 1255, em Cambrai, Nord, Hauts-de-France, França, seu pai, Raoul de Falvy, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Marguerite, tinha 36 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Marie de Bazentin. Ele faleceu em Falvy, Somme, Hauts-de-France, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ecuyer Pierre I de Quincy et de Montauban родился в 1255 г. в местности камбреи, норд, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция, его отцу по имени Raoul de Falvy было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Marguerite, 36. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Marie de Bazentin. Он умер в местности фалви, сомм, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ecuyer Pierre I de Quincy et de Montauban nació en 1255, en Cambrai, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Francia, su papá, Raoul de Falvy, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Marguerite, tenía 36 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Marie de Bazentin. Murió en Falvy, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia.
